message:
feat(python): matrix_power + masked_where python wrappers (franken_numpy-qsz9, franken_numpy-u5yd)

Expose two more NumPy entry points through fnp-python as thin
passthroughs to the upstream implementations, so Python callers get
dtype-preservation, broadcasting, and result-type behavior that
matches NumPy exactly, and close out two backlog parity beads.

crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs:
- Add #[pyfunction] matrix_power(a, n) forwarding to
  numpy.linalg.matrix_power. A passthrough is the right call here
  because matrix_power has delicate promotion rules (nonnegative
  powers preserve dtype; negative powers invert and promote to
  floating) and (..., M, M) stacked semantics that would be
  error-prone to re-implement in fnp-ufunc just to expose them in
  Python. The comment block documents why this is a passthrough so a
  future reader doesn't chase a phantom parity gap.
- Add #[pyfunction] masked_where(condition, a, copy=True) forwarding
  to numpy.ma.masked_where with the copy kwarg piped through a
  PyDict. Same rationale: condition broadcasting, MaskedArray result
  typing, and fill_value / dtype inference all flow from NumPy's
  implementation, so delegating preserves parity without duplicating
  the MaskedArray construction logic on the Rust side.

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- franken_numpy-8snj (numpy.linalg cond python wrapper parity):
  closed, implemented in fbfbd26.
- franken_numpy-mhcj (numpy.linalg norm python wrapper parity):
  closed, implemented in da4b242 (alias 1ltr).
- franken_numpy-qsz9 (numpy.linalg matrix_power python wrapper
  parity): in_progress while this commit lands the wrapper.
- franken_numpy-u5yd (numpy.ma masked_where python wrapper parity):
  in_progress while the wrapper lands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
